text.title: Cost-vs-Quality Decision-Log Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn a decision log from institutional memory into compliance theater. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Re-litigation — a trade considered and rejected 6 months ago is re-proposed without referencing the prior entry.
failure-mode.detector: New proposal mentions decision X for feature Y AND a prior entry with same feature + same change exists with status reverted/ratified
failure-mode.repair: Surface the prior entry before discussion. The trade may still be made (conditions changed), but it MUST be made aware of the prior outcome, not in ignorance.

failure-mode.description: Retroactive entries — log only documents trades after they pay off, hiding the failed ones.
failure-mode.detector: All entries have observed_impact = positive AND no entries have status = reverted
failure-mode.repair: Backfill reverted decisions (look at git history for restored model/prompt configs). The log loses calibration if it only shows wins.

failure-mode.description: Missing baseline — entry doesn't capture pre-change cost/quality, so observed impact can't be measured.
failure-mode.detector: Entry lacks pre-change cost AND quality numbers OR the linked provenance doesn't have them
failure-mode.repair: Add baseline section to log-entry template. For new entries, baseline is captured at decision-time from monitoring. For old entries, mark observed_impact as "unmeasurable" and learn for future.

failure-mode.description: Rollback trigger written as vague intent ("if quality drops"), never actually monitored.
failure-mode.detector: Trigger lacks a numeric threshold OR is not implemented as an alert/check
failure-mode.repair: Either set a numeric threshold and wire an alert OR drop the entry as un-controlled trade (and re-decide with a real trigger).

failure-mode.description: Status stays "active" forever — review-after date passes, no one closes the loop.
failure-mode.detector: log-review-overdue.py shows entries past review-after date by > 30 days
failure-mode.repair: Weekly review ritual: walk overdue list, force each to ratified/reverted/superseded with a one-line observed_impact note. Backlog is a sign the review cadence is unowned.

failure-mode.description: Log diverges from production — a change shipped to prod is not logged, or a log entry exists without a deployed change.
failure-mode.detector: Audit: cross-reference recent prod config diffs vs new log entries; mismatches flagged
failure-mode.repair: Tie log-entry creation to deploy pipeline (e.g., a PR template that requires linking a dec-id). Audit quarterly.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: If an LLM is drafting log entries, observed-impact summaries must cite monitoring data, not narrate them. Hallucinated metrics ("quality improved by 3%") with no link are silently rewriting history. Reject.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly the team lead reviews the log: how many entries reverted, how many ratified, any patterns. Patterns inform process (e.g., consistent over-estimation of quality gap suggests adjusting eval rubric). The log is an audit tool only if it's audited.
